assertions.title: Trade Calendar Covers 2021 Through 2026

steps.action: sqlite3 data/market.db "SELECT MIN(date), MAX(date) FROM trade_calendar;"

steps.observed: Code correctly has start_date = '20210101', but database only has 2026 data — calendar update not re-run after code fix

assertions.issues: Trade calendar only spans 2026-01-01 to 2026-12-31. The code in calendar_updater.py correctly sets start_date='20210101', but the database has not been updated with the full 2021-2027 range. The update_trade_calendar() function needs to be executed to populate the calendar.

assertions.title: Trade Calendar Contains Reasonable Row Count

steps.action: sqlite3 data/market.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM trade_calendar WHERE is_trading_day = 1;"

steps.expected: >= 2,000 total rows (trading + non-trading days over 6 years)

assertions.issues: Only 242 trading days in the calendar (365 total rows for 2026 only). Expected 1,300-1,700 trading days across a 6-year range. Root cause is the same as VAL-INFRA-003: the calendar has not been repopulated after the code change to extend start_date to 2021.

assertions.title: INDEX_LIST Includes CSI 300, CSI 500, and CSI 1000

steps.observed: All 5 original indices confirmed present in INDEX_LIST at positions 1-5

blockers.description: Trade calendar has not been repopulated after the code change to extend start_date to 2021. The code correctly sets start_date='20210101' in calendar_updater.py, but the database still only contains 2026 data. The update_trade_calendar() function needs to be executed to fix VAL-INFRA-003 and VAL-INFRA-004.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Read-only validation — did not attempt to run update_trade_calendar() as that would modify the database

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-INFRA-005, VAL-INFRA-006), 2 failed (VAL-INFRA-003, VAL-INFRA-004). INDEX_LIST correctly includes all 8 indices (5 original + 3 new CSI indices). Trade calendar only covers 2026 despite code being updated to start from 2021 — the calendar update function has not been re-executed against the database.
